stories.title: Anthropic’s new Claude feature is quietly selling you on AI
stories.simple_summary: Anthropic (a company that makes Claude, an AI chatbot) added a new dashboard that shows you how much you use their AI tool. This display is designed to make you think about how much you've come to rely on it.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mostly a marketing trick — nothing you need to act on, but be aware that companies use these reminders to get you more attached to their products.

stories.title: Anthropic, OpenAI, and SpaceX are bigger than the last 25 years of tech exits
stories.simple_summary: Three major AI companies are about to go public (sell shares on the stock market), and together they'll be worth more money than 25 years of all other tech company sales combined.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors and people in the stock market — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: Meta says its new AI model is ready to compete on coding
stories.simple_summary: Meta (Facebook's parent company) released a new AI tool that can help programmers write computer code, and they're making it available for other companies to use.
stories.why_it_matters: This matters mainly to software developers and tech companies — not something the average person or small business owner needs to worry about.

stories.title: Say hello to Claude Wrapped
stories.simple_summary: Claude (an AI chatbot) now has a feature like Spotify Wrapped that shows you how you used the AI over the past month.
stories.why_it_matters: If you use Claude, you can now see a year-end summary of your activity — similar to how Spotify shows you your favorite songs.

stories.title: Character.AI wants a piece of the microdrama pie
stories.simple_summary: Character.AI (a company that makes chatbots) is now creating short video shows that you can watch and chat with the characters in them, like interactive TV.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mostly for entertainment and investors — not something that affects your daily life or business.

stories.title: Popular open source AI developer tool Ollama raises $65M, grows to nearly 9M users
stories.simple_summary: Ollama, a free tool that lets people run AI programs on their own computer instead of using a company's website, raised $65 million and now has almost 9 million users.
stories.why_it_matters: If you're tech-savvy or a programmer, this tool gives you more control over AI without relying on big companies — but most small-business owners won't use it.

stories.title: Character.AI enters the microdrama arena with its own productions, but there’s a twist
stories.simple_summary: Character.AI is making TV-like shows where you can pause and chat with the characters or change what happens in the story.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mostly for entertainment — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: FL Studio 2026 turns its AI chatbot into your assistant engineer
stories.simple_summary: FL Studio (music-making software) upgraded its AI helper to not just answer questions, but actually assist you while you're making music, like having a smart assistant in the program.
stories.why_it_matters: This only matters if you make music with FL Studio — otherwise it doesn't affect you.

stories.title: Nandan Nilekani leaves GP role at Fundamentum as it launches $200M third fund
stories.simple_summary: A business leader in India is stepping back from one role at an investment company as that company raises $200 million to invest in AI and financial-tech startups.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors and people in India's tech industry — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: Lovable reportedly in talks to double its valuation to $13.2B
stories.simple_summary: A company called Lovable is raising $300 million and may become worth $13.2 billion based on investor interest.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors and people tracking the AI startup market — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: Google’s deepfake detector system used to debunk McConnell hoax pic
stories.simple_summary: Google created a tool that can spot AI-generated fake images, and it was used to prove that a photo of a senator was actually a fake made by AI.
stories.why_it_matters: This is good news — as AI-generated fakes become more common, having tools to detect them helps protect people from being tricked by false information.

stories.title: SpaceXAI releases Grok 4.5, which Elon describes as an ‘Opus-class model’
stories.simple_summary: Elon Musk's company released a new AI model called Grok 4.5 that's supposed to be cheaper and work faster than other powerful AI tools.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors and tech companies looking for cheaper AI options — it doesn't change anything for the average person or small business owner.
